calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Continued To Dec-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Linda A. Otieno, administrator in pro per (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Settlement of Insolvent Estate, First and Final Account and Report, Allowance for Statutory Compensation.

Discussion was held regarding Ms. Otieno's prior legal representation by Mr. Dada. The Court noted that it was concerned about counsel's prior involvement, referencing invoices submitted by the petitioner that indicated assistance in administering the decedent's probate estate.

The Court confirmed of disclaimers that had been filed and noted that the only remaining estate asset was the decedent's separate property business, valued at approximately $175,000. Ms. Otieno clarified that the original location closed following eviction, and the business had been re-established at a new location.

The Court asked whether the decedent had siblings. Ms. Otieno stated there was one sibling who was aware of the matter. The Court explained that, given the disclaimers, parental interests pass to the sibling, and an assignment of rights is required to properly close the estate. The Court suggested that Ms. Otieno obtain assistance from the Access Center or the individual who helped prepare the disclaimers.

The Court advised that statutory attorney fees for the estate total $6,250 upon closure and noted the invoices reflected substantially greater charges. The Court instructed the petitioner to address this with her former counsel.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-16-2025 Continued To Nov-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Linda Otieno, Administrator in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Codi M. Dada, former attorney for administrator (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on the Petition for Settlement of Insolvent Estate, First and Final Account and Report, Allowance for Statutory Compensation.

The Court noted that disclaimers had recently been filed, but one filing appeared incomplete, consisting only of a caption page. The Court directed Ms. Otieno to check with the Clerk's Office to determine whether the original filing had been improperly scanned.

The Court inquired into invoices from Mr. Dada. Mr. Dada reported that he had billed Ms. Otieno on an hourly basis and that she had paid his fees from personal funds, not from the estate.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to November 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Ms. Otieno was ordered to file the invoices from Mr. Dada by September 30, 2025. The Court excused Mr. Dada from further appearances unless otherwise ordered.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

POST-SCRIPT:

Following the hearing, the Clerk's Office re-scanned the incomplete disclaimer filed on 9/15/2025 (discussed above). A complete copy was provided to Judge Murphy by the courtroom clerk.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Continued To Sep-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Linda Otieno, Administrator in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Codi M. Dada, former attorney to Ms. Otieno (via Zoom).

Mr. Dada responded to allegations raised by Ms. Otieno. He stated that he was retained under a written hourly fee agreement and was paid directly by Ms. Otieno from her personal funds. He denied receiving any funds from the estate and explained that he resigned as counsel due to ethical concerns arising from inconsistent information provided by Ms. Otieno and his discomfort with how she managed the administration.

Ms. Otieno responded that she had informed Mr. Dada of all business-related developments and disputes, and that some payments to him were in fact made from estate funds. She denied attempting to extort or mislead Mr. Dada and claimed that she had followed his advice throughout the proceeding. Both parties offered differing accounts regarding the handling of business assets, payments, and the administration process.

The Court noted that a review of the invoices submitted by Mr. Dada would be necessary and confirmed that there is still a need for consents or waivers from the decedent's parents. Ms. Otieno indicated that she had attempted to contact them but had been unsuccessful so far.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court directed Ms. Otieno to file consents or waivers from the decedent's parents as soon as possible. Mr. Dada was instructed to appear on the continued date for further inquiry into his billing and role in the administration.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees

Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Continued To Apr-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Continued To Jan-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-29-2024 Continued To Dec-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Codi Dada, Esq., for and with Linda Otieno, Administrator.

At the court's query, the Administrator and counsel offer an update regarding the delay in closing the subject Estate. The court advises that since the issues have yet to be resolved, even though counsel was advised of the issues in May of this year, the court needs a formal petition and is inclined to continue this matter to allow one to be filed. The court also advises that it is considering imposing sanctions if the issues are not resolved prior to the next hearing. Counsel offers argument against the court's plan to impose sanctions and requests to be removed as attorney of record. The court advises counsel that it is unable to remove him as requested and reminds him that he would need to formally request to be removed.

At the conclusion of discussion, the subject matter is continued to Dec-11-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel is instructed to address the numerous issues in this matter by Nov-27-2024.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Wong. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Continued
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Codi Dada, Esq., for and with Linda Otieno, Administrator.

Dennis D. Miller, Esq, for 1424-1428 Polk, I2, LLC, Creditor.

Counsel and Ms. Otieno offer the court an update regarding the status of this matter. The court encourages the Administrator to work with Mr. Miller to resolve the estate funds issue.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jan-24-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file an accounting petition by Jan-10-2024. If an accounting is not filed by the court's deadline, the court will consider sanctions.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Continued To Feb-06-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Codi Dada appeared by zoom for Administrator.

Mr. Dada stated that he was informed this morning that the administrator has resolved all of the tax issues, that they received the bankruptcy order which shows that the creditor's claim by 1424 -1428 Polk, LLC was 95% discharged, that there are only three months outstanding/unaccounted for, and that the administrator said she has receipts/a letter showing that those three months have been paid. Mr. Dada also stated that they are hoping to resolve all of the creditor issues, that they should be able to file a final petition within 60-days, and that they filed a petition, but it did not take because they put a hearing date on it. Status on close is continued to February 6, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Either a petition for final distribution , or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed by January 23, 2023. The court advised Mr. Dada not to put a date on it.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Continued To Nov-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Codi Dada, via Zoom, for Linda A. Otieno, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports of delays in closing this matter due to pending confirmation from the CA Franchise Tax Board and IRS regarding resolution of alleged tax debts and of pending confirmation of status on creditor's claims which may have been discharged via bankruptcy proceedings. Counsel advises that he had submitted to the court a formal report with the above information, but it had been rejected because today's date was indicated in the pleading's caption.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Nov-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Codi Dada, via Zoom, for Linda Otieno, Personal Representative.

Mr. Dada reports that his client has filed taxes and that there is a slight delay with a landlord requesting for payment of items that have been discharged via bankruptcy proceedings but that he expects to file a final petition and requests a continuance. The court confirms that the decedent was an only child. The court advises counsel that it is granting a continuance with a caveat that he needs to file either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve status of administration prior to the next status hearing date.

The matter is continued to Oct-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Codi Dada, via Zoom, for Linda Otieno, Personal Representative.

Mr. Dada reports of delays in closing the estate caused by pending creditor's claims but that his client is currently working on negotiating those claims and asks for a sixty-day continuance. The court points counsel to the issue of the Inventory and Appraisal does not characterize whether the property is community or separate property. Mr. Dada reports that the decedent's parents have disclaimed so all of the estate's property will be distributed to his client. Mr. Dada advises that he intends to file a petition for final distribution once the creditor's claims have been resolved.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-03-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Continued To Jul-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Codi Dada, via Zoom, for Linda Otieno, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports that his client had been out of the country for three months but has returned. Counsel advises that he has an Inventory and Appraisal ready for a signature and that he intends to file a Petition for Final Distribution.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jul-06-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2021 Continued To May-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Codi Dada, via ZOOM, counsel for Linda Otieno, Petitioner.

At the outset, Mr. Dada requests an extension of special powers and advises that waiver of bond from the parents can be filed within 30 days. The court queries Mr. Dada regarding notice of this hearing to the parents. Mr. Dada advises that a notice of hearing was e-filed on MAR-19-2021 which is confirmed by the court. The court intends to extend the Letters once the appropriate papers are received to extend and issue the updated Letters. The court advises Mr. Dada to try and work with the Examiner assigned to this matter. After brief discussion, the petition for letters of Administration is continued to MAY-05-2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.
